oids.name: zxAnGponOPTcontTable
oids.description: The ONU profile T-CONT table.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPTcontEntry
oids.description: The ONU profile T-CONT entry.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPTcontIndex
oids.description:
An arbitrary (unique) integer for identifying an entry
             in the table, from 1 to upper limit supported T-CONT count of
             ONU type.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPTcontName
oids.description: Name of the T-CONT.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPTcontBwPrfName
oids.description: The name of profile used by T-CONT bandwidth configuration.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPTcontDbaGapMode
oids.description:
The DBA gap mode. 
             For GPON ONU, the explanations of these modes are as follows:
               mode0 is one frame, 
               mode1 is two frames, 
               mode2 is four frames, 
               mode3 is eight frames.
               default is the system default policy.
             For example, 'two frames' indicates that T-CONT will perform DBA 
             in a gap of two data frames.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPTcontRowStatus
oids.description:
The status of this conceptual row.
        
             To create a row in this table, a manager must
             set this object to createAndGo(4).

             To delete a row in this table, a manager must
             set this object to destroy(6).
             
             To query this object, always return 1.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPGemPortTcontIndex
oids.description:
It is equal to the 'zxAnGponOPTcontIndex' 
             of 'zxAnGponOPTcontTable'.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPGemPortQueueOfTcont
oids.description:
The queue (index from 0 to 7) of T-CONT for this GEM port.
             Queue 7 is the highest priority queue.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPOnuConfTable
oids.description: The ONU profile ONU configuration table.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPOnuConfEntry
oids.description: The ONU profile ONU configuration entry.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPOnuConfVportMode
oids.description:
The VPort mode.
              gemport(1): a related VPort will be created automatically when a 
                          GEM port is created.
              manual(3): VPort will be created manually.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPOnuConfUsFecEnable
oids.description:
Enables or disables the ONU upstream FEC function.
             enabled(1): enable the ONU upstream FEC function.
             disabled(2): disable the ONU upstream FEC function.
             auto(3): system will enable or disable the ONU upstream FEC 
                      function automatically according to the system automation 
                      rule.
             default(4): system will enable or disable the ONU upstream FEC 
                      function according to the system default rule.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPTcontQueueSchedTable
oids.description: The ONU profile T-CONT queue schedule table.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPTcontQueueSchedEntry
oids.description: The ONU profile T-CONT queue schedule entry.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPTcontQueueSchedPrfName
oids.description:
The T-CONT queue schedule profile name, this profile is 
             defined in zxAnGponRmTrafficSchedPrfTable. The empty string
             indicates that no profile is bound to this T-CONT.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPWanOnuIdentifier
oids.description:
A unique ONU identifier string. This string, if set is provided as
             part of the DHCP request to allow an alternative to MAC Address in
             retrieving the DHCP parameters of the specified ONU.
             This attribute is related to 'zxAnGponOPWanIpMode'.
             and it is valid only when 'zxAnGponOPWanIpMode' is DHCP.
             Its default value is null bytes.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVoipIpOnuIdentifier
oids.description:
This attribute indicates the ONU identifier string. 
             This attribute is relation to 'zxAnGponOPVoipIpMode'. 
             This attribute is valid only when 'zxAnGponOPVoipIpMode' is DHCP. 
             A unique ONU identifier string. If set to a non-null value,
             this string is used instead of the MAC address in retrieving
             DHCP parameters. If the string is shorter than 25 characters,
             it must be null terminated. Its default value is null bytes.

oids.name: zxAnPonOnuTypeTable
oids.description: Configuration of ONU type.

oids.name: zxAnPonOnuTypeEntry
oids.description:
Entry of zxAnPonOnuTypeTable.
            For the default ONU type such as ZTE-F641,ZTE-F622,
            ZTE-F625, ZTE-F621, ZTE-F601, ZTE-F640, ZTE-F628, ZTE-9806,
            ZTE-D400, ZTE-D402, ZTE-D420, ZTE-D421, ZTE-D422, ZTE-F401,
            ZTE-F420, ZTE-F425, ZTE-F429, ZTE-F430, ZTE-F435, ZTE-F500,
            ZTE-F803, ZTE-F820, ZTE-F821, ZTE-F822, set operation is prohibited.        
                    
            The multi-variables binding rule should be complied with 
            when a manager configures ONU type. According to this rule, 
            all the related mib variables must be included in one SNMP 
            set operation. 
            
            There are two types of variables: mandatory and optional.
            All of the mandatory variables must be included in one SNMP
            set operation. Optional variables may be included in one 
            SNMP set operation with the mandatory variables, but must 
            not be used alone. 
            
            To create or modify ONU type, the mandatory variables are as 
            follows:
            mandatoty: zxAnPonOnuTypePonType.

oids.name: zxAnPonOnuTypeName
oids.description: Name of ONU type.

oids.name: zxAnPonOnuTypePonType
oids.description:
PON type.There are two types:
            -epon: Ethernet Passive Optical Network
            -gpon: Gigabit-Capable PON.

oids.name: zxAnPonOnuTypeDesc
oids.description:
Description of ONU type.
             eg. the interface attributes of ONU type.
             This variable must comply with the multi-variables binding 
             rule described in zxAnPonOnuTypeEntry.

oids.name: zxAnPonOnuTypeAttr1
oids.description:
For EPON: This attribute indicates protect type,
                       1-none, 2-typeC, 3-typeD, default value is 1.
             For GPON: This attribute indicates the maximum number of T-CONT, 
                       default value is 255.
             This variable must comply with the multi-variables binding 
             rule described in 'zxAnPonOnuTypeEntry'.

oids.name: zxAnPonOnuTypeAttr2
oids.description:
For EPON: This attribute indicates speed,
                       1-1G, 2-10G Symmetric, 3-10G Asymmetric, 
                       default value is 1.
             For GPON: This attribute indicates the maximum number of GEM port,
                       default value is 255.
             This variable must comply with the multi-variables binding 
             rule described in 'zxAnPonOnuTypeEntry'.

oids.name: zxAnPonOnuTypeAttr3
oids.description:
For EPON: This attribute indicates ONU auto dispatch enable status,
                       1-enable, 2-disable, default value is 1.
             For GPON: This attribute indicates maximum number of switch per 
                       slot, default value is 255.
             This variable must comply with the multi-variables binding 
             rule described in 'zxAnPonOnuTypeEntry'.

oids.name: zxAnPonOnuTypeAttr4
oids.description:
For GPON: This attribute indicates maximum number of flow per 
                       switch, default value is 255.
             This attribute is not used for EPON.            
             This variable must comply with the multi-variables binding 
             rule described in 'zxAnPonOnuTypeEntry'.

oids.name: zxAnPonOnuTypeAttr5
oids.description:
For GPON: This attribute indicates maximum number of IP host.
             MSB : Most Significant Byte
             LSB : Least Significant Byte
        
             |      MSB     |    Byte 2    |    Byte 1       |    LSB          |
             |--------------|--------------|-----------------|-----------------|
             |              |              |IPv6 host number | IPv4 host number|
             |     (8bits)  |  (8bits)     |    (8bits)      |     (8bits)     |
             |-----------------------------------------------------------------|
             
             IPv4 host default number is 2. IPv6 host default number is 0.
             This attribute is not used for EPON.            
             This variable must comply with the multi-variables binding 
             rule described in 'zxAnPonOnuTypeEntry'.

oids.name: zxAnPonOnuTypeAttr6
oids.description:
For GPON: This attribute indicates service ability of ONU type,
            there are three service mode :N:1,1:M,1:P.
            If support the service mode,set the corresponding bit to 1,
            if not support, set to 0.   
            Default value is 7.               
                 
            | MSB                                  LSB| 
            |-----|-----------|-----------|-----------|
            | ... |    1:P    |    1:M    |    N:1    |
            | ... | (1bits)   | (1bits)   | (1bits)   |
            +-----+-----------+-----------+-----------+
            This attribute is not used for EPON.
            This variable must comply with the multi-variables binding 
            rule described in 'zxAnPonOnuTypeEntry'.

oids.name: zxAnPonOnuTypeAttr7
oids.description:
The OLT OMCI management mode. 
            |      MSB       |    Byte 2    |    Byte 1    |      LSB     |
            |-------------------------------------------------------------|
            |    Reserved    |   Reserved   |   Reserved   |Reserved WiFi |
            |     (8bits)    |   (8bits)    |    (8bits)   |(7bits) (1bit)|
            |-------------------------------------------------------------|
            Bits 1 - 31 are reserved for future use. 
            Bit 0 represents WiFi service, default value is 0.
            If the bit is set to 1, WiFi service is managed via non-OMCI.                        
            If the bit is set to 0, WiFi service is managed via OMCI.            
            This attribute is not used for EPON.

oids.name: zxAnPonOnuTypeAttr8
oids.description:
A set of capabilities information about ONU.            
            | MSB |Byte2|Byte1|               LSB                                       |
            |---------------------------------------------------------------------------|
            |Rese-|Rese-|Rese-|Rese-|  GIS    |Extended|       |      |Multicast| OMCI  |
            |rved |rved |rved |rved |Function |  OMCI  | MGC   | VRG  | Address |Sending|
            |(8bi-|(8bi-|(8bi-|(1bi-| Enabling|Enabling| Mode  |      |  Range  | Mode  |
            | ts) | ts) | ts) | ts)-|(1bit)   | (1bit) |(2bits)|(1bit)| (1bit)  |(1bit) |
            |-----------------------  --------------------------------------------------|
            Bits 7 - 31 are reserved for future use.            
            Bit 0 represents OMCI sending mode, default value is 0.
            If the bit is set to 1, OLT sends OMCI by synchronous mode.             
            If the bit is set to 0, OLT sends OMCI by asynchronous mode.  
             
            Bit 1 represents default multicast address range mode,
            default value is 0.            
            If the bit is set to 1, default multicast address range is
            all-inclusive mode(224.0.0.0-239.255.255.255).                                   
            If the bit is set to 0, default multicast address range is none. 
            
            Bit 2 represents VRG enabling status, default value is 0.        
            If the bit is set to 1, VRG function of the ONU is enabled.            
            If the bit is set to 0, VRG function of the ONU is disabled.            
            This attribute is not used for EPON.
            
            Bit 3 and bit 4 represent MGC configuration mode, default value 
            is 0. If both of two bits are set to 0, the MGC configuration mode 
            is private. If bit 3 is set to 1 and bit 4 is set to 0, the MGC
            configuration mode is ITU-T. If bit 3 is set to 0 and bit 4 is set 
            to 1, the MGC configure mode is both.

            Bit 5 represents extended OMCI enabling status, default value is 0.
            If the bit is set to 1, extended OMCI is enabled.
            If the bit is set to 0, extended OMCI is disabled.            
            This attribute is not used for EPON.
            
            Bit 6 represents PON GIS function is supported or not, 
            default value is 0.
            If the bit is set to 1, GIS function is supported.
            If the bit is set to 0, GIS function is not supported.

oids.name: zxAnPonOnuTypeAttr9
oids.description:
A set of capabilities information about ONU.
            |      MSB       |    Byte 2    |    Byte 1    |      LSB     |
            |-------------------------------------------------------------|
            |    Reserved    |   Reserved   |   Reserved   |  VEIP Number |
            |     (8bits)    |   (8bits)    |    (8bits)   |    (8bits)   |
            |-------------------------------------------------------------|
            The LSB represents the number of VEIP port. The value 0 represents
            the system default.
            This attribute is not used for EPON.

oids.name: zxAnPonOnuTypeAttr10
oids.description:
A set of capabilities information about ONU.
            |      MSB       |    Byte 2    |    Byte 1    |      LSB          |
            |------------------------------------------------------------------|
            |    Reserved    |   Reserved   |   Reserved   |Reserved|Speed Mode|
            |     (8bits)    |   (8bits)    |    (8bits)   | (4bits)|(4bits)   |
            |------------------------------------------------------------------|
            Bits 4 - 31 are reserved for future use.            
            Bits 0 - 3 represents the ONU speed mode, 
                 1 - GPON, 2 - XG-PON, 3 - XGS-PON, 4 - automatic, 
                 the default value is 4.
            This attribute is not used for EPON.

oids.name: zxAnPonOnuTypeRowStatus
oids.description:
The status of this conceptual row.
             
            The supported actions of this conceptual row only include:
            'createAndGo' and 'destroy'.
             
            To create a row in this table, a manager must
            set this object to createAndGo(4).
                 
            In particular, a newly created row cannot be created
            until the corresponding zxAnPonOnuTypeName
            and zxAnPonOnuTypePonType have been set.
    
            To delete a row in this table, a manager must
            set this object to destroy(6). 
            
            To get this object, the agent always returns active(1).

oids.name: zxAnPonOnuIfAttrTable
oids.description: Configuration of ONU interface attributes.

oids.name: zxAnPonOnuIfAttrEntry
oids.description:
Entry of zxAnPonOnuIfAttrTable.
             Entries in the zxAnPonOnuIfAttrTable are created and
             deleted using the zxAnPonOnuIfAttrRowStatus object,
             modify is prohibited.

oids.name: zxAnPonOnuIfId
oids.description:
The most significant 16 bits indicate the type of this interface:
            ETH(10), E1(20), T1(21), DS1(22), POTS(30), RF(40), WiFi(50), 
            COM(60), VEIP(70), xDSL(80). 
            The least significant 16 bits indicate the ID of this interface:
            the first 8 bits specifies the slot ID associated with this UNI,
            the second 8 bits specifies the port ID associated with this UNI.
            eg.the zxAnPonOnuIfId for eth_0/1 is 655361(0xA0001).

The status of this conceptual row.

            To create a row in this table, a manager must

            To get this object, the agent always returns active(1).

oids.name: zxAnPonOnuIndex
oids.description: This attribute represents the index of ONU.

oids.name: zxAnPonRogueOnuOltIndex
oids.description:
Same as ifIndex:
             |-------------+----------|------------|---------|---------|
             |InterfaceType| rack No. |  Shelf No. | Slot No.| Port No.|
             |  1 (4bits)  | (4bits)  |  (8bits)   | (8bits) | (8bits) |
             +-------------+----------+------------+---------+---------+       
             
             The multi-variables binding rule should be complied with 
             when a manager configures rogue ONU control. 
             According to this rule, all the related mib variables must be 
             included in one SNMP set operation. 
             
             There are two types of variables: mandatory and optional.
             All of the mandatory variables must be included in one SNMP
             set operation. Optional variables may be included in one 
             SNMP set operation with the mandatory variables, but must 
             not be used alone. 
             
             To configure rogue ONU control, the mandatory and optional 
             variables are as follows:
             mandatoty: zxAnPonRogueOnuOltIndex,
                        zxAnPonRogueOnuIndex

oids.name: zxAnPonRogueOnuIndex
oids.description: Represents the normal ONU index.

oids.name: zxAnPonRogueOnuState
oids.description:
This object indicates the control action.
            -rogueOn:open the transmit power.
            -rogueOff:close the transmit power.
            -shutDown:shutdown the transceiver.

oids.name: zxAnPonRogueOnuRecoverTime
oids.description:
This object indicates the recovery time.
            When 'zxAnPonRogueOnuState' is 'rogueOn',
            'zxAnPonRogueOnuRecoverTime' is invalid.

oids.name: zxAnPonRogueOnuDetectInfoTable
oids.description: This table is defined to control rogue ONU.

oids.name: zxAnPonRogueOnuDetectInfoEntry
oids.description:
Entry of zxAnPonRogueOnuDetectInfoTable.  
            
             The multi-variables binding rule should be complied with 
             when a manager configures rogue ONU detect. 
             According to this rule, 
             all the related mib variables must be included in one SNMP 
             set operation. 
             
             There are two types of variables: mandatory and optional.
             All of the mandatory variables must be included in one SNMP
             set operation. Optional variables may be included in one 
             SNMP set operation with the mandatory variables, but must 
             not be used alone. 
             
             To configure rogue ONU detect, the mandatory and 
             optional variables are as follows:
             mandatoty: zxAnRogueOnuDetectEnable,
                        zxAnRogueOnuLocatingEnable,
                        zxAnRogueOnuAutoCloseEnable.

oids.name: zxAnRogueOnuSlot
oids.description: The OLT slot to be configured rogue ONU detection.

oids.name: zxAnRogueOnuDetectEnable
oids.description:
This object indicates the rogue ONU detection mode.
            -enabled(1): enable the rogue ONU detection  
            -disabled(2): disable the rogue ONU detection.

oids.name: zxAnRogueOnuLocatingEnable
oids.description:
This object indicates the rogue ONU locating mode.
            -enabled(1): locating the rogue ONU
            -disabled(1): not locating the rogue ONU.
            When 'zxAnRogueOnuDetectEnable' is 'enabled', you can configure
            'zxAnRogueOnuLocatingEnable'. 
            When 'zxAnRogueOnuDetectEnable' is 'disabled', you can not 
            configure 'zxAnRogueOnuLocatingEnable'.
            This object is not supported for C600 serial.

oids.name: zxAnRogueOnuAutoCloseEnable
oids.description:
This object indicates the rogue ONU shutdown mode.
            -enabled(1): shutdown the rogue ONU automatically
            -disabled(2): shutdown the rogue ONU manually
            When 'zxAnRogueOnuLocatingEnable' is 'enabled', you can configure
            'zxAnRogueOnuAutoCloseEnable'. 
            When 'zxAnRogueOnuLocatingEnable' is 'disabled', you can not 
            configure 'zxAnRogueOnuAutoCloseEnable'.
            This object is not supported for C600 serial.

oids.name: zxAnPonRogueOnuInfoTable
oids.description: This table is defined to get rogue ONU information.

oids.name: zxAnPonRogueOnuInfoEntry
oids.description:
It is an entry in the zxAnPonRogueOnuInfoTable. The index 
                 'ifIndex' represents the normal OLT index

oids.name: zxAnPonRogueOnuInstIndex
oids.description: The instance No. of the rogue ONU.

oids.name: zxAnPonRogueOnuAuthStatus
oids.description:
The rogue ONU authentication status, 'authenticated' means this ONU 
            is authenticated  and 'unauthenticated' means this ONU is discovered 
            by OLT but not authenticated.

oids.name: zxAnPonRogueOnuId
oids.description:
The unique ONU ID of the specified PON port, 
             value 0 means ONU is discovered by OLT but not authenticated.

oids.name: zxAnPonRogueOnuMacAddress
oids.description: The MAC address of the rogue ONU, only for EPON ONU.

oids.name: zxAnPonRogueOnuSerialNumber
oids.description: The SN of the rogue ONU, only for GPON ONU.

oids.name: zxAnPonRogueOnuDetectedTime
oids.description: The date and time when the rogue ONU is detected.

oids.name: zxAnPonPortRogueOnuConfTable
oids.description:
The PON port rogue ONU management table.
             This table only is used for C600 serial.

oids.name: zxAnPonPortRogueOnuConfEntry
oids.description: The PON port rogue ONU management entry.

oids.name: zxAnPortRogueOnuLocatingEnable
oids.description:
The rogue ONU locating mode.
             enabled(1): locating the rogue ONU
             disabled(2): not locating the rogue ONU.

oids.name: zxAnPortRogueOnuAutoCloseEnable
oids.description:
The rogue ONU shutdown mode.
             enabled(1): shutdown the rogue ONU automatically
             disabled(2): shutdown the rogue ONU manually.

oids.name: zxAnPortRogueOnuDetectEnable
oids.description:
The rogue ONU detection mode.
             enabled(1) enable the rogue ONU detection function 
             disabled(2): disable the rogue ONU detection function.

oids.name: zxAnPonGisHorizontalError
oids.description:
The horizontal error of the PON equipment. It caused by the 
             changing of horizontal location during the measurement.

oids.description: PON ONU interface statistics table

oids.name: zxAnPonOnuIfRxPkts

oids.name: zxAnPonOnuIfRxPktRate
oids.description: Received packets per second.

oids.name: zxAnPonOnuIfRxOctetPeakRate
oids.description: Peak rate of received octets.

oids.name: zxAnPonOnuIfRxPktPeakRate
oids.description: Peak rate of received packets.

oids.name: zxAnPonOnuIfRxUcastPkts
oids.description: Received unicast packets.

oids.name: zxAnPonOnuIfRxNUcastPkts
oids.description: Received non-unicast packets.

oids.name: zxAnPonOnuIfRxMcastPkts
oids.description: Received multicast packets.

oids.name: zxAnPonOnuIfRxBcastPkts
oids.description: Received broadcast packets.

oids.name: zxAnPonOnuIfRxDiscardPkts
oids.description: Received discarded packets.

oids.name: zxAnPonOnuIfRxErrPkts
oids.description: Received errored packets.

oids.name: zxAnPonOnuIfRxPdr
oids.description: Received packets drop ratio, in 10^-9 granularity.

oids.name: zxAnPonOnuIfRxPer
oids.description: Received packets error ratio, in 10^-9 granularity.

oids.name: zxAnPonOnuIfRxCrcAlignErrors
oids.description:
The total number of packets received that
            had a length (excluding framing bits, but
            including FCS octets) of between 64 and 1518
            octets, inclusive, but but had either a bad
            Frame Check Sequence (FCS) with an integral
            number of octets (FCS Error) or a bad FCS with
            a non-integral number of octets (Alignment Error).

oids.name: zxAnPonOnuIfRxUndersizePkts
oids.description:
The total number of packets received that were
            less than 64 octets long (excluding framing bits,
            but including FCS octets) and were otherwise well
            formed.

oids.name: zxAnPonOnuIfRxOversizePkts
oids.description:
The total number of packets received that were
            longer than 1518 octets (excluding framing bits,
            but including FCS octets) and were otherwise
            well formed.

oids.name: zxAnPonOnuIfRxPkts64Octets
oids.description:
The total number of packets (including bad
            packets) received that were 64 octets in length
            (excluding framing bits but including FCS octets).

oids.name: zxAnPonOnuIfRxPkts65To127Octets
oids.description:
The total number of packets (including bad
            packets) received that were between 65 and 127 octets 
            in length (excluding framing bits but including FCS octets).

oids.name: zxAnPonOnuIfRxPkts128To255Octets
oids.description:
The total number of packets (including bad
            packets) received that were between 128 and 255 octets 
            in length (excluding framing bits but including FCS octets).

oids.name: zxAnPonOnuIfRxPkts256To511Octets
oids.description:
The total number of packets (including bad
            packets) received that were between 256 and 511 octets in length
            (excluding framing bits but including FCS octets).

oids.name: zxAnPonOnuIfRxPkts512To1023Oct
oids.description:
The total number of packets (including bad
            packets) received that were between 512 and 1023 octets in length
            (excluding framing bits but including FCS octets).

oids.name: zxAnPonOnuIfRxPkts1024To1518Oct
oids.description:
The total number of packets (including bad
             packets) received that were between 1024 and 1518 octets in length
             (excluding framing bits but including FCS octets).

oids.name: zxAnPonOnuIfRxBwUtilization
oids.description: Received bandwidth throughput.

oids.name: zxAnPonOnuIfRxAvgBwUtilization
oids.description: Received average bandwidth utilization.

oids.name: zxAnPonOnuIfRxFragments
oids.description:
The total number of packets received that were less than
         64 octets in length (excluding framing bits but including
         FCS octets) and had either a bad Frame Check Sequence
         (FCS) with an integral number of octets (FCS Error) or a
         bad FCS with a non-integral number of octets (Alignment
         Error).

         Note that it is entirely normal for zxAnPonOnuIfRxFragments
          to increment.  This is because it counts both runts (which are
         normal occurrences due to collisions) and noise hits.

oids.name: zxAnPonOnuIfRxJabbers
oids.description:
The total number of packets received that were
         longer than 1518 octets (excluding framing bits,
         but including FCS octets), and had either a bad
         Frame Check Sequence (FCS) with an integral number
         of octets (FCS Error) or a bad FCS with a non-integral
         number of octets (Alignment Error).

         Note that this definition of jabber is different

oids.name: zxAnPonOnuPerfAlmPrfConfTable
oids.description: The performance alarm threshold profile table of interfaces.

oids.name: zxAnPonOnuPerfAlmPrfConfEntry
oids.description:
An entry in zxAnPonOnuPerfAlmPrfConfTable.
            When a manager wants to create or modify an ONU performance 
            alarm threshold item,it must comply with the multi-variables 
            usage rule that all the mib variables related to same 
            fuction must be in one snmp set operation, the variables have
            only one type: mandatory.
            
            Mandatory variables must be in the one snmp set operation.
                   
            To create an ONU performance alarm threshold item on
            different level, alarm threshold ,restore threshold and 
            zxAnPonOnuPerfAlmConfRowStatus should be in one snmp set operation.
            The alarm threshold and restore threshold variables in the 
            one snmp set operationshould be on same level.
            Such as:
            zxAnPonOnuPerfRiseAlmThresh and zxAnPonOnuPerfRiseAlmClrThresh,
            zxAnPonOnuPerfRiseWarnThresh and zxAnPonOnuPerfRiseWarnClrThresh,
            zxAnPonOnuPerfFallWarnThresh and zxAnPonOnuPerfFallWarnClrThresh,
            zxAnPonOnuPerfFallAlmThresh and zxAnPonOnuPerfFallAlmClrThresh.
                          
            To modify an ONU performance alarm threshold item on
            different level,alarm threshold and restore threshold
            should be also in the one snmp set 
            operation regardless of modifying alarm or restore threshold 
            value alone.
            The alarm threshold and restore threshold variables in the 
            one snmp set operationshould be on same level.
            Such as:
            zxAnPonOnuPerfRiseAlmThresh and zxAnPonOnuPerfRiseAlmClrThresh,
            zxAnPonOnuPerfRiseWarnThresh and zxAnPonOnuPerfRiseWarnClrThresh,
            zxAnPonOnuPerfFallWarnThresh and zxAnPonOnuPerfFallWarnClrThresh,
            zxAnPonOnuPerfFallAlmThresh and zxAnPonOnuPerfFallAlmClrThresh.
            
            To remove an ONU performance alarm threshold item, only 
            zxAnPonOnuPerfAlmConfRowStatus should be set to destroy(6) while
            all levels of this ONU performance alarm threshold item will be 
            removed.

oids.name: zxAnPonOnuPerfAlmPrfApplyEntry
oids.description:
An entry in zxAnPonOnuPerfAlmPrfApplyTable.
            The index 'zxAnPonOnuIndex' represents the normal ONU index.
            When a manager wants to apply an ONU performance alarm 
            profile to specificed ONU interface,it must comply with 
            the multi-variables usage rule that all the mib variables 
            related to same fuction must be in one snmp set operation, 
            the variables have only one type: mandatory.
            
            Mandatory variables must be in the one snmp set operation.
                   
            To apply an ONU performance alarm profile to the specificed ONU 
            interface, 'zxAnPonOnuPerfAlmPrf' and 
            'zxAnPonOnuAlmPrfApplyRowStatus' should be in the one snmp set 
            operation.
            
            To remove the application of an ONU performance alarm profile 
            to the specificed ONU interface, 
            only 'zxAnPonOnuAlmPrfApplyRowStatus' should be set to destroy(6).
